title: cassia fastuosa var. calva
intro: Cassia fastuosa var. calva, also known as the Naked Cassia or Senna, is a plant species within the Legume family, characterized by its yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and laxative effects. The health benefits of Cassia fastuosa var. calva include treating constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as providing relief from fever and skin conditions. Cassia fastuosa var. calva can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to support overall well-being.
